case_name: Marc Chartier, Individually, and as Administrator of the Estate of Lisa Chartier v. Apple Therapy of Londonderry, LLC & a.

summary.brief: Marc Chartier, Individually, and as Administrator of the Estate of Lisa Chartier v. Apple Therapy of Londonderry, LLC & a.. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: On January 5, 2018, Dr. Killie performed surgery on Lisa’s left knee. On that same day, Lisa went home in a long leg brace.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Lisa had an elevated risk of developing deep vein thrombosis (DVT) following surgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Within the next two days, Lisa developed calf soreness in her left leg. Calf soreness is a sign of DVT.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On January 12, Lisa began physical therapy at Apple Therapy. She reported symptoms of DVT at this visit, but nothing was done to detect DVT.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On January 15, Lisa went to her first postoperative visit with Dr. Killie. At that appointment, Lisa informed Dr. Killie about her calf soreness.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Killie told Lisa that the soreness 'was a normal effect of the surgery,' and said 'to keep doing what she was doing.'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On January 17, Lisa went to her second physical therapy appointment at Apple Therapy. She reported to her physical therapist that she was still experiencing calf soreness.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: After the appointment, Marc drove Lisa home and went to work.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Around 7:00 p.m. on January 17, Marc and Lisa left home to go to a brewery. After making her way to the car using crutches, Lisa expressed that she was 'a little bit out of breath.'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: During the drive home, her condition quickly deteriorated. Lisa told Marc that she felt hot, and she began making terrible noises associated with her breathing before passing out twice in the passenger seat of the car.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Marc drove to a nearby urgent care facility, but it was closed. He then called 911.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: At the hospital, care providers performed chest compressions on Lisa. Marc stood in the threshold of the room where care providers attempted to resuscitate her.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The care providers were unsuccessful in their efforts to save Lisa’s life, and she passed away at 11:15 p.m.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Following Lisa’s death, the medical examiner determined that she died from 'cardiovascular collapse due to pulmonary thromboembolism due to [DVT] due to immobilization following [the] recent surgical repair of [her] torn meniscus.'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The trial court granted the defendants’ motion concluding that, as a matter of law, 'the time between the negligent act and [Marc’s] injury is simply too attenuated to recover for [negligent infliction of emotional distress].'
evidence_index.speaker: Trial court conclusion

evidence_index.text: We hold that the trial court erred in granting summary judgment to the defendants on the basis that Marc’s emotional distress was too attenuated from the defendants’ negligent conduct to permit recovery.
evidence_index.speaker: Supreme Court reasoning

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Lisa developed calf soreness in her left leg within two days post-surgery, a sign of DVT. She reported these symptoms during her physical therapy and postoperative visits.
past_medical_history: Elevated risk of DVT following knee surgery

focused_exam.lower_extremity: Calf soreness reported, but no further examination details documented

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Potential DVT due to postoperative calf soreness

reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt further imaging if elevated

requestables.name: Venous Doppler Ultrasound
requestables.description: Imaging to detect DVT in the lower extremities

reveal.detailed_findings: Imaging performed but findings not detailed in judgment
reveal.clinical_significance: Could confirm or rule out DVT
reveal.decision_impact: Positive finding would require anticoagulation therapy

timeline_phases.name: Postoperative Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient reports symptoms during follow-up and physical therapy visits

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient reports calf soreness post-knee surgery
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in managing the patient's symptoms?

options.clinical_reasoning: Standard procedure to rule out DVT in patients with calf soreness post-surgery

options.description: Reassure patient and advise to continue current activities
options.clinical_reasoning: Assumed soreness was a normal postoperative effect

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant reassured patient and advised to continue current activities
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant stated soreness was a normal effect of surgery

expected_action_court.description: Venous Doppler Ultrasound should have been ordered
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established imaging is standard for suspected DVT

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to investigate potential DVT despite symptoms
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Imaging should have been performed to rule out DVT

patient_outcome.description: Patient died from cardiovascular collapse due to pulmonary thromboembolism

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to investigate DVT symptoms
Causation established - earlier intervention could have prevented death

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to order appropriate diagnostic tests for DVT
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Imaging would have confirmed DVT, allowing for timely treatment

factual_timeline.event: Patient's condition deteriorated, leading to death

allegations.allegation: Failure to diagnose and treat DVT
